pattern_description: BROKEN DAG (REVERSED EDGE): Identical to DAG #9 except the edge between Smoking and Lung Cancer is reversed (Lung Cancer → Smoking). Tests whether the LLM can detect causally implausible directions based on domain knowledge — cancer cannot cause someone to start smoking.
difficulty_notes: Critique task. The flaw is detectable through domain reasoning rather than graph structure alone. A strong model should flag the reversed edge and explain why it contradicts known mechanisms.
description: A modified version of the Asia network with a deliberately reversed causal edge: this graph claims that Lung Cancer causes Smoking, which is biologically implausible.

explanation_points: This graph contains a reversed causal edge
Lung Cancer cannot cause someone to start smoking — the temporal and biological direction is opposite

counterfactual.intervention: Not applicable — the graph contains a structural error
counterfactual.expected_outcome: Counterfactual reasoning on this graph would produce misleading results because of the reversed edge

flaws.explanation: Smoking is a well-established cause of lung cancer. The reverse direction is biologically implausible — having lung cancer does not cause people to take up smoking. A correct DAG should have Smoking → Lung Cancer.
